editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to Pembroke Wicks, Private Secretary to Edward Carson (1854-1935).

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.placeName: 25 Victoria Street, Westminster, London, S.W., England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D1507/A/17/32
118 TELEPHONE
4503 VICTORIA. The following
returned to
the U.D.L. 25, Victoria Street,
Westminster, S.W. 28th June 1916 Dear Mr. Wicks, I have your letter of June 27th. I am sorry
but I must ask you to return the cuttings which were
stamped Union Defence League as I have no duplicates
of them: and Mr. Cambray would be very angry if he knew
that our file was not complete. They are: —
Belfast News Letters September 4th, 1914 — No. A7114 Carson Belfast Recruits Women
Belfast News Letters September 5th, 1914 — No. A7123 Carson Recruiting Women
Belfast News Letters September 7th, 1914 — No. A7147 Coleraine
Belfast News Letters September 9th, 1914 — No. A7391 Bonar Law Carson Belfast
Morning Post September 15th, 1914 — No. A7228 Carlton Club Faithfully Yours
Rosa E. Omash
Pembroke Wicks, Esq,. 1,
Garden Court,
Temple, E.C.
Returned 29.6.16
